subject: Human Resources
title: Servant Leadership
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Servant Leadership.

hook.text: A CEO carries chairs for an event setup. Staff watch in disbelief. 'I'm here to help you succeed,' she says.

buildup.text: Servant leadership inverts the hierarchy—leaders exist to support, develop, and remove obstacles for their teams.

discovery.text: Robert Greenleaf coined the concept in 1970, arguing the best leaders serve first and lead second.

twist.text: Servant leadership isn't weakness. It requires the courage to put others' growth ahead of personal status.

climax.text: Teams with servant leaders report higher engagement, trust, and willingness to go above and beyond.

punchline.text: The best leaders don't ask 'who works for me?' but 'who do I work for?'
